title: Education advisers and school inspectors
description: Develop instructional material, coordinate educational content, and incorporate current technology into instruction in order to provide guidelines to educators and instructors for developing curricula and conducting courses. May train and coach teachers. Includes educational consultants and specialists, and instructional material directors.

tasks.task: Conduct or participate in workshops, committees, and conferences designed to promote the intellectual, social, and physical welfare of students, and address public audiences to explain program objectives and to elicit support.

Plan and conduct teacher training programs and conferences dealing with new classroom procedures, instructional materials and equipment, and teaching aids, including teaching instructors to use instructional technology or to integrate technology with teaching.
Research, evaluate, and prepare recommendations on curricula, instructional methods, and materials for school systems, and recommend changes to curricula or delivery methods based on various data.

Observe work of teaching staff to evaluate performance and recommend changes to strengthen teaching skills, and advise teaching and administrative staff in curriculum development and implementation of state and federal programs.

Provide analytical support for the design and development of training curricula, learning strategies, educational policies, or courseware standards, and develop instructional materials such as lesson plans, handouts, or examinations.
Assess effectiveness and efficiency of instruction, analyzing performance data to determine the effectiveness of instructional systems and materials.
Conduct needs assessments and strategic learning assessments to develop the basis for curriculum development or to update curricula, and define instructional, learning, or performance objectives.

Coordinate activities of workers engaged in cataloging, distributing, and maintaining educational materials and equipment in curriculum libraries and laboratories, and prepare or approve manuals, guidelines, and reports on state educational policies and practices.
Update the content of educational programs to ensure that students are being trained with equipment and processes that are technologically current.
Create measurement tools to evaluate the effectiveness of instruction or training interventions and develop master course documentation or manuals as per accreditation or certification requirements.

Design learning products, including Web-based aids or electronic performance support systems, instructional aids for classroom or online use, and adapt instructional content or delivery methods for different levels of learners.
Research and evaluate emerging instructional technologies or methods and present and make recommendations regarding course design, technology, and instruction delivery options.
Recommend, order, or authorize the purchase of instructional materials, supplies, equipment, and visual aids designed to meet student educational needs and district standards.

detailed_work_activities.title: Advise educators on curricula, instructional methods, or policies.
Research topics in area of expertise.
Modify teaching methods or materials to accommodate student needs.
Enforce rules or policies governing student behavior.
Teach others to use technology or equipment.
Serve on institutional or departmental committees.
Advise students on academic or career matters.
Order instructional or library materials or equipment.

alternate_titles: E-Learning Instructional Designer (Electronic Learning Instructional Designer)
